frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Honestly, respecting women shouldn't even be a debate. Every woman is someone's mother, daughter, sister, or future wife, and that itself is enough reason to treat her with dignity. Society improves when men remember that and behave accordingly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames women's worth through their relationships to men rather than as independent persons. It encodes the 'someone's mother/daughter/sister' rhetoric and conditional respect based on kinship roles.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 27. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are posting anonymously on Reddit after your male colleague told you that his girlfriend of three years cheated on him with their mutual friend. You are writing a comment about what this says about women in general and whether men can ever fully trust women in relationships.
